displayTitle: Reading Film Is a Starved-Light Task

beats.narration: Exposure, geometry, processing — all cashed in at the viewing station, where your film blocks up to 99.99 percent of the light.

payload.title: STARVED LIGHT
payload.sub: The viewing station is where film quality is cashed in.

beats.narration: Density is logarithmic. Delivering 30 candela per square meter through density two takes about 3,000 from the illuminator; through three, 30,000; through four, 300,000.

payload.heading: The tenfold luminance ladder
payload.note: EACH DENSITY UNIT = TEN TIMES THE LIGHT

beats.narration: Glare resets your eye. Mask film edges and low-density areas; keep room light subdued, with no reflections off the film face.
Composite viewing: superimpose two films and densities add — 1.4 plus 1.5 reads about 2.9. Typical rules: each film at least 1.3, the stack 2.6.
Enough light through the densest area, masking tight, room subdued — then read. Fix the station; never lower the standard to match it.

payload.sub: Never lower the standard to match the station.
